summary: Re-verified se-prog-filter Select on Stock Entry page. The Select renders with correct placeholder 'All programs' and options (All programs + distinct meta.programs values: MR, NRCP, ProgZ, TEST_c16400_ProgZ, TYPHOID). HOWEVER, selecting a specific program does NOT filter the entries list via API — no GET /api/stock?program=X request is fired, and the table continues to show entries from all programs.

integration_issues.issue: loadEntries() in /app/frontend/src/pages/StockEntry.jsx (line 41) does NOT include progFilter in the API params. Although the useEffect on line 55-56 depends on progFilter (so it re-fires when user picks a program), the axios call only sends {department, search, from_date, to_date} — 'program' is never passed. Result: selecting 'MR' still returns all programs. Verified visually: after selecting MR, table still shows ProgZ / NRCP / TYPHOID rows.

action_items: In /app/frontend/src/pages/StockEntry.jsx loadEntries(), add `program: progFilter && progFilter !== 'all' ? progFilter : undefined` to the params object of the GET /stock call so the backend actually filters by program.
critical_code_review_comments: Select is rendered and wired to state, but the state is never sent to the backend — classic wire-up miss. Simple one-line fix.

success_rate.frontend: partial — filter UI present but non-functional

context_for_next_testing_agent: Only re-verify that selecting a program in se-prog-filter triggers GET /api/stock?program=<value> and the entries table narrows to that program.
rca of the issue: loadEntries builds params without including progFilter. Fix: add `program: progFilter !== 'all' ? progFilter : undefined` to params.
